candidate_summary.operating_rule: Candidate score prioritizes immediate sales action: waiting/anchorage + long idle + high-value vessel + regulated destination + known operator.

gdrive_archive.error: Service Accounts do not have storage quota. Leverage shared drives (https://developers.google.com/workspace/drive/api/guides/about-shareddrives), or use OAuth delegation (http://support.google.com/a/answer/7281227) instead.

data_mode_detail.label: STATIC SNAPSHOT
data_mode_detail.message: Dashboard has at least one configured source group. Verify collector output before treating it as live operating data.

keep_repository_light: Do not commit heavy raw archives
Archive bulky raw data to Google Drive/Supabase

api_sources.label: Supabase Database
api_sources.use: Store vessel snapshots, pipeline reports, dashboard history, and future lead intelligence tables.

api_sources.label: MOF Vessel Specification
api_sources.use: Vessel particulars, IMO/MMSI enrichment, ship type, size class, and sales segmentation.

api_sources.label: Pilot Schedule Sources
api_sources.use: Pilotage schedule URLs used for port-call and movement confirmation where allowed.

api_sources.label: Berth Schedule Sources
api_sources.use: Public berth/source URLs used for berth assignment, waiting vessels, and terminal watch.

api_sources.label: Port Facility API
api_sources.use: Port/berth/facility master data for Korean port intelligence normalization.

api_sources.label: Port Operation API
api_sources.use: Arrival/departure, port operation, and vessel movement enrichment.

api_sources.label: Ulsan Port Core API
api_sources.use: Ulsan berth detail, cargo plan, berth operation, and terminal process feeds.

api_sources.label: MOF VTS API
api_sources.use: VTS-based port movement and port-code scoped vessel traffic monitoring.

api_sources.label: MOF AIS Dynamic API
api_sources.use: Dynamic AIS position, speed, heading, and recent vessel movement snapshots.

api_sources.label: MOF AIS Vessel Info API
api_sources.use: AIS vessel info enrichment including vessel identity and static particulars.

api_sources.label: MOF AIS Statistics API
api_sources.use: AIS traffic statistics for congestion baselines and port trend reporting.

api_sources.label: MarineTraffic AIS
api_sources.use: Optional external AIS fallback for live vessel position, ETA, speed, and port call data.

api_sources.label: VesselFinder AIS
api_sources.use: Optional secondary AIS feed for vessel movement and berth/anchorage watch.

api_sources.label: AISStream
api_sources.use: Streaming AIS signal when the project moves from snapshot mode to live monitoring.

api_sources.label: OpenWeather
api_sources.use: Weather and sea-condition context for biofouling and operation risk scoring.

api_sources.label: Google Maps / Geocoding
api_sources.use: Port coordinates, map layers, geocoding, and dashboard routing enhancements.

api_sources.label: Korea Public Data Fallback
api_sources.use: Generic Korean public-data fallback when source-specific keys are not configured.

api_sources.label: Email Alerts
api_sources.use: Daily high-risk vessel alerts and sales action summaries.

api_sources.label: Vercel Deployment
api_sources.use: Automated production deployment from GitHub Actions.

api_sources.label: Google Drive Archive
api_sources.use: Archive daily reports and raw data exports outside the repository.

data_strategy.principle: Use Korean public/port/MOF sources as the operating base. Treat MarineTraffic/VesselFinder/AISStream as optional paid enrichment, not a blocker.
data_strategy.vts_architecture: Integrated VTS / national vessel traffic layer. Yeosu is one monitored area, not the core architecture.
source_priority: Integrated VTS / national vessel traffic information
next_focus: Normalize vessel identity across port, berth, VTS and AIS feeds
Accumulate daily snapshots in Supabase for idle-time and port-stay history
Keep paid AIS integrations disabled unless a customer requires global real-time coverage

collector_readiness.name: Korea port-call base layer
collector_readiness.goal: Confirm arrivals, berth assignment, waiting status, and port-call timing without paid AIS.

collector_readiness.name: Vessel identity enrichment
collector_readiness.goal: Normalize IMO/MMSI, vessel type, size class, operator, and target segment.

collector_readiness.name: Movement / idle-time signals
collector_readiness.goal: Detect anchorage, low speed, long stay, berth shifts, and port congestion signals.

collector_readiness.name: Trend and reporting history
collector_readiness.goal: Accumulate daily snapshots for sales timing, repeat calls, and pipeline reporting.

collector_readiness.name: Paid AIS enrichment
collector_readiness.goal: Use only when global real-time coverage becomes commercially justified.

collector_manifest.business_use: Korea arrivals/departures, port-call timing, and initial sales target discovery.
Berth assignment, waiting status, terminal movement, and short-window outreach timing.
collector_manifest.next_action: Run a smoke test and inspect normalized output rows.

collector_manifest.business_use: Low-speed, anchorage, idle-time and movement confirmation without paid AIS dependency.
collector_manifest.next_action: Run a smoke test and inspect normalized output rows.

collector_manifest.business_use: Vessel type, size class, identity merge, and opportunity segmentation.
collector_manifest.next_action: Run a smoke test and inspect normalized output rows.

collector_manifest.business_use: Keep GitHub light while accumulating repeated snapshots for port-stay and lead history.
collector_manifest.next_action: Run a smoke test and inspect normalized output rows.

collector_manifest.business_use: Commercial-only enrichment when a customer or pilot project requires global real-time coverage.
collector_manifest.next_action: Configure or validate: marine_traffic, vesselfinder, aisstream

source_registry.weight_guidance: Keep collector outputs small in GitHub. Store raw/heavy archive data in Supabase or Google Drive.
source_registry.immediate_focus: Start collector smoke tests and normalization rules.

cloud_master_db.principle: Use GitHub only for code and small dashboard snapshots. Use Supabase as the queryable master DB and Google Drive/Object Storage as the raw archive layer.
recommended_architecture: Collectors fetch public/port/MOF data on schedule
Normalizer maps each source into common vessel, port-call and event fields
Supabase stores normalized master tables and daily snapshots
Raw bulky payloads are archived externally and referenced by URI
Dashboard reads compact JSON exports or direct Supabase views

master_tables.role: Arrival, berth, pilot, departure and status events
Daily accumulated operating snapshot for idle-time and trend analysis
Biofouling, idle, compliance and sales opportunity scoring history
API success, failure, fallback and last-sync telemetry
Pointer table for heavy raw files stored outside GitHub

accumulation_policy.daily_snapshot: append-only by date; never overwrite historical rows
accumulation_policy.port_call_events: append events, then deduplicate by source + vessel + port + timestamp window
accumulation_policy.raw_data: store only compressed/raw archive pointers in DB; do not commit raw bulk to GitHub
accumulation_policy.retention: keep lightweight dashboard JSON in repo, keep full historical archive in cloud storage

development_order: Create Supabase master tables and indexes
Add idempotent upsert/append writers for normalized rows
Add source_health logging for every collector run
Backfill history gradually from existing data/history snapshots

release_cadence.policy: Bundle five to seven small improvements into one stable minor build instead of releasing every tiny patch.
current_bundle: GitHub Actions trigger fix and scheduled update stabilization
Backend snapshot and cloud master DB operating guidance
release_cadence.next_bundle_rule: Only cut the next minor build after 5-7 meaningful backend/data improvements are ready, unless a blocking hotfix is required.
release_cadence.hotfix_rule: Use patch-only release when GitHub Actions, build, validation, or dashboard rendering is broken.
stability_guardrails: Keep heavy raw data out of GitHub
Use public/MOF/port APIs before paid AIS
Do not trust candidate counts when data_mode is sample_only
Show candidate numbers only with source and freshness context

seven_pack_summary.delivery_policy: Ship one stable ZIP after grouping five to seven validated improvements.
improvements: Candidate operations center: 24h queue, confidence buckets, port focus
Backend health score: source coverage, sample/live ratio, blockers and warnings
Candidate priority rank and stale-data guard per vessel
Workflow secret coverage expanded for detailed Ulsan/MOF public API keys
Validation strengthened for candidate, backend and workflow outputs
Release cadence updated from three-patch bundles to seven-pack stability releases
Dashboard labeling updated so the user can distinguish operating candidates from sample-mode tests
seven_pack_summary.stability_guard: No schema-breaking DB migration is required in this release; it remains compatible with existing data JSON outputs.

runtime_budget.collector_policy: Run lightweight public-data collectors first; skip slow optional sources; never block dashboard generation.
runtime_budget.paid_ais_policy: MarineTraffic/VesselFinder/AISStream stay optional and should not block Korea candidate detection.
runtime_budget.failure_policy: If a collector fails or times out, preserve latest generated snapshot/fallback data and write the error into pipeline-report/status.

storage_layers.role: queryable vessel snapshots, candidate history, port-stay history
large raw API payloads and source files in GDrive/Object Storage

next_schema_targets: candidate_events: new candidate, score jump, became immediate, dropped
source_runs: collector success/failure, row counts, latency and error text
master_db_roadmap.migration_guard: Do not require a destructive migration yet; add append-only tables and views first.

next_development_plan.title: Keep build lightweight
next_development_plan.detail: Do not add heavy raw archives to GitHub. Keep dashboard JSON small and push raw/history data to Supabase or GDrive.

next_development_plan.title: Connect public collectors first
next_development_plan.detail: Prioritize PORT_OPERATION, BERTH/PILOT URLs, MOF AIS/VTS and Ulsan sources before paid AIS.

next_development_plan.title: Replace sample rows gradually
next_development_plan.detail: At least one source group is configured; next work is normalization and duplicate control.

next_development_plan.title: Build cloud master DB
next_development_plan.detail: Supabase is available; next step is normalized master tables and append-only daily snapshots.

next_development_plan.title: Separate master DB from raw archive
next_development_plan.detail: Supabase should store queryable normalized data; Google Drive/Object Storage should hold heavy raw payloads and source files.

backend_priorities: Run lightweight collectors first; do not block the dashboard when a source fails.
Normalize vessel identity before scoring; use IMO/MMSI when available and vessel name only as fallback.
Append daily/hourly snapshots to Supabase; keep GitHub limited to current dashboard JSON.
Separate paid AIS from public-data base layer so cost does not block Korea candidate discovery.
next_backend_tasks: Wire the first real public collector to produce normalized vessel rows.
Persist hourly candidate snapshots to Supabase with upsert keys.
Add source-level error reporting per collector.
Add port-stay duration calculation from repeated snapshots.

candidate_changes.action_rule: Prioritize vessels that newly became immediate candidates, then major score jumps, then new candidates. Use this list for first-call planning before reviewing the full queue.

backend_stability_batch.release_policy: Accumulate up to seven backend/data improvements and ship one validated ZIP instead of many tiny patches.
seven_improvements: Backend release cadence changed to batched stable releases
Candidate count guarded by sample/live data mode
Public-data-first source readiness separated from paid AIS readiness
Storage readiness separated into Supabase and raw archive lanes
Runtime budget and timeout policy documented for collectors
Master DB evolution path clarified before heavy data ingestion
Validation now checks backend stability batch outputs
backend_stability_batch.operating_note: Use current candidate counts as operational candidates only after data_mode is not sample_only and freshness is acceptable.

candidate_ops.recommended_daily_action: No live cleaning candidate signal yet; check collector status and sample/live data mode.
candidate_ops.live_data_warning: Candidate count can be used as an operating signal after checking freshness/source health.

next_backend_moves: Run collectors in smoke-test mode before replacing sample rows
Write normalized snapshots to Supabase using idempotent upsert/append rules
Generate candidate counts from the latest successful snapshot only
Keep GitHub output compact and archive heavy raw payloads externally

commercial_command_center.focus_question: Which vessel should HullWiper Korea contact now, and why?
commercial_command_center.operating_rule: Load hot vessels first for mobile speed. Load full vessels only when the operator expands the full queue.

checks.label: Static dashboard files generated
checks.detail: dashboard/index.html and public/index.html should exist for hosting.

checks.label: Hosting output directory
checks.detail: For Vercel/Netlify, set build command to npm run build and output directory to public or dashboard depending on routing.

checks.label: Existing API secrets detected
checks.detail: 12 API group(s) enabled. The pipeline will use configured sources and keep fallback data for missing sources.

checks.label: Collector readiness roadmap
checks.detail: Enough source groups are configured for the next collector connection pass.

checks.label: AIS / vessel tracking source
checks.detail: AIS source detected for vessel movement enrichment.

checks.label: Sample/live data guard
checks.detail: Dashboard has at least one configured source group. Verify collector output before treating it as live operating data.

gdrive.error: Service Accounts do not have storage quota. Leverage shared drives (https://developers.google.com/workspace/drive/api/guides/about-shareddrives), or use OAuth delegation (http://support.google.com/a/answer/7281227) instead.
